project.name: Cortex
project.description: Local-only personal command center that aggregates projects, deadlines, TODOs, and knowledge into a single web dashboard on localhost.

architecture_decisions.code_location: Brain2 repo IS the dashboard ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â reads SecondBrain paths via .env config (not embedded in vault)
architecture_decisions.package_structure: Single package with concurrently for dev (frontend + backend)
architecture_decisions.state_file_detection: Priority: agent_state.md ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ Agent_State.json ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ state.md ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ Status.md ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ README.md
architecture_decisions.status_inference: From frontmatter status: field, or content keywords (blocked, completed, in progress, stale threshold)
architecture_decisions.google_calendar: P1 ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â new Google Cloud project, guided OAuth2 setup

external_dependencies: Claude API (optional ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â AI summarization and TODO extraction)
Google Calendar API (P1 ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â OAuth2, read-only)
Obsidian vault (plain markdown, read by dashboard)

recent_decisions: Brain2 repo is the dashboard (not embedded in Obsidian vault)
Claude API only (no local LLM abstraction)
Google Calendar deferred to P1 with guided OAuth setup
Create Obsidian vault from scratch with PARA structure

project_brief: Cortex ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â local-only personal command center for Adi. Aggregates all projects from C:\Users\boomb\Documents\_Projects (scans state files), extracts TODOs from all markdown, shows deadlines from deadlines.md with urgency timeline, and provides quick capture to inbox.md. React+Vite+Tailwind frontend on :5173, Express.js TS backend on :3001. No database ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â all filesystem. P1: Google Calendar sync, Claude API summaries, chat export viewer.

TASK-001.title: Scaffold project (Vite + Express + Tailwind + TS)
TASK-001.description: Initialize pnpm project with React+Vite+TypeScript frontend, Express.js TypeScript backend, Tailwind CSS, concurrently dev script. Create .env template with PROJECTS_DIR, VAULT_DIR, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, PORT. Set up project directory structure: src/ for frontend, server/ for backend.
acceptance_criteria: pnpm dev starts both Vite (:5173) and Express (:3001) concurrently
TypeScript compiles cleanly for both frontend and backend
Tailwind CSS is configured and working
.env.example exists with all required variables
Frontend can make API calls to backend (CORS configured)

TASK-002.title: Create Obsidian vault PARA structure + templates
TASK-002.description: Create Obsidian vault at C:\Users\boomb\Documents\SecondBrain with full PARA structure (Inbox/, Projects/, Areas/, Resources/, Archive/, ChatExports/, Deadlines/, DailyNotes/). Seed deadlines.md with template format, create inbox.md, create symlinks from Projects/ to _Projects/* subfolders using mklink /D.
acceptance_criteria: All PARA directories exist under SecondBrain/
deadlines.md exists with correct format (- [ ] YYYY-MM-DD | Description | tag)
inbox.md exists with header
Symlinks from Projects/ to _Projects/* work on Windows

TASK-003.title: Backend: Project scanner & state file parser
TASK-003.description: Implement project scanner that recursively scans PROJECTS_DIR for state files with priority detection (agent_state.md ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ Agent_State.json ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ state.md ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ Status.md ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ README.md). Parse markdown/JSON for status, summary, next-steps. Infer status from content keywords. Calculate stale thresholds (14d amber, 30d red). Expose via GET /api/projects.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/projects returns array of project objects
Each project has: name, path, stateFile, status, lastModified, summary, nextSteps, staleDays
State file priority detection works correctly
Status inference from content keywords works when no frontmatter
Stale thresholds calculated correctly
Tests pass for scanner with mock filesystem

TASK-004.title: Backend: TODO extractor with checkbox write-back
TASK-004.description: Implement TODO extractor that scans all markdown files in PROJECTS_DIR and VAULT_DIR for unchecked tasks (- [ ]), checked tasks (- [x]), and TODO:/FIXME:/HACK: comments. Group by source project. Expose via GET /api/todos. Implement PATCH /api/todos/:id to toggle checkbox in source file (write-back).
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/todos returns total, completed, byProject breakdown
Extracts - [ ], - [x], TODO:, FIXME:, HACK: patterns
PATCH /api/todos/:id toggles checkbox in source markdown file
Write-back doesn't corrupt file content
Tests pass for extraction and write-back

TASK-005.title: Backend: Deadline reader & urgency calculation
TASK-005.description: Implement deadline reader that parses VAULT_DIR/Deadlines/deadlines.md. Parse format: - [ ] YYYY-MM-DD | Description | optional-tag. Calculate urgency: red (<48h), amber (<7d), green (later), gray (completed). Expose via GET /api/deadlines sorted by date.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/deadlines returns sorted array of deadline objects
Each deadline has: date, description, tag, done, urgency level
Urgency calculation correct for all thresholds
Handles completed (- [x]) deadlines as gray
Tests pass for parsing and urgency calculation

TASK-006.title: Backend: Quick capture endpoint + notes corpus parser
TASK-006.description: Implement POST /api/capture that appends timestamped entry to VAULT_DIR/Inbox/inbox.md in format: - [ ] [YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm] Text. Also implement a one-time parser for the existing notes file at C:\Users\boomb\Documents\notes_corpus.txt.txt ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â extract any actionable items and make them available via the TODO API.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/capture appends to inbox.md with correct format
Timestamp is accurate
File is created if it doesn't exist
Notes corpus parser extracts items from existing file
Tests pass for capture and file creation

TASK-007.title: Frontend: Dashboard layout, project cards, status overview
TASK-007.description: Build the main dashboard layout with Tailwind. Implement ProjectCard component showing: name, status badge (color-coded), last touched date, 2-3 line summary, 'Open in VS Code' button. Implement StatusOverview stats panel (active projects, open TODOs, stale count). Grid layout sorted by last-modified.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard loads and displays project cards from API
Cards show status badge, last modified, summary, next steps
Open in VS Code button triggers correct protocol link
Stale indicators: amber >14d, red >30d
Stats panel shows counts
Responsive grid layout

TASK-008.title: Frontend: TODO aggregator with mark-done
TASK-008.description: Build TodoAggregator component. Display all TODOs grouped by project with total/per-project counts. Flat list sortable by project or file. Checkbox to mark done (calls PATCH /api/todos/:id, updates UI optimistically).
acceptance_criteria: Displays all TODOs from API grouped by project
Shows total and per-project counts
Sortable by project or file
Checking a TODO calls the backend and updates UI
Optimistic update with error rollback

TASK-009.title: Frontend: Deadline timeline component
TASK-009.description: Build DeadlineTimeline component. Vertical timeline layout, color-coded by urgency (red <48h, amber <7d, green later, gray done). Show date, description, and tag. Completed deadlines shown at bottom in gray.
acceptance_criteria: Displays deadlines from API as vertical timeline
Color coding matches urgency thresholds
Completed deadlines in gray at bottom
Tags displayed as chips/badges

TASK-010.title: Frontend: Quick capture input bar
TASK-010.description: Build QuickCapture component. Persistent text input at top of dashboard. Type thought/TODO, hit Enter, calls POST /api/capture. Clear input on success, show brief confirmation. Keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+K) to focus.
acceptance_criteria: Input bar at top of dashboard
Enter submits and clears input
Calls POST /api/capture with correct payload
Shows brief success/error feedback
Ctrl+K focuses the input

TASK-011.title: Integration, polling, error states, E2E testing
TASK-011.description: Wire all components together. Add 60-second polling interval for auto-refresh. Add loading states, error boundaries, empty states. End-to-end testing with actual filesystem. Verify full success criteria from PRD.
acceptance_criteria: All components render together in dashboard layout
60-second polling refreshes data
Loading skeletons during fetch
Error states displayed gracefully
Empty states for zero projects/todos/deadlines
Page load under 2 seconds
Full PRD success criteria met

TASK-012.title: ntfy push notifications (deadlines + stale + digest)
TASK-012.description: Implement POST /api/notify/test and background notification service. Use public ntfy.sh topic (configurable via NTFY_TOPIC in .env). Triggers: (1) red deadlines ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â check on server start + every 60min, notify if any due within 2 days and not yet notified today; (2) stale projects ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â notify when project crosses 30-day threshold; (3) daily digest ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â configurable time via NTFY_DIGEST_TIME (default 08:00), sends summary of open todos count + upcoming deadlines. Store last-notified state in VAULT_DIR/.cortex-notify-state.json to prevent duplicate alerts.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/notify/test sends a test notification to ntfy topic
Red deadline notification fires when deadline.urgency = red
Stale project notification fires when staleDays crosses 30
Daily digest sent at configured time
No duplicate notifications (per-day dedup)
NTFY_TOPIC and NTFY_DIGEST_TIME in .env.example
Graceful no-op when NTFY_TOPIC not set

TASK-013.title: Ollama AI summarization (llama3.1:8b, auto on load)
TASK-013.description: Implement GET /api/ai/summarize/:project using local Ollama API (http://localhost:11434). Model: llama3.1:8b. On dashboard load, fetch summaries for all active projects automatically. Prompt: given the state file content, return a 2-3 sentence 'where did I leave off?' summary. 1-hour in-memory cache keyed by project name + file mtime. Graceful fallback: if Ollama not running, return null and show nothing (no error UI). GET /api/ai/status returns whether Ollama is reachable.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/ai/summarize/:project returns 2-3 sentence summary
Summaries auto-fetch on dashboard load for active projects
1-hour cache prevents redundant Ollama calls
GET /api/ai/status returns { available: boolean }
Graceful no-op when Ollama not running (no UI error)
Model configurable via OLLAMA_MODEL in .env (default llama3.1:8b)
OLLAMA_URL in .env.example (default http://localhost:11434)

TASK-014.title: Chat export viewer (import, search, tag)
TASK-014.description: Import Claude conversation exports (JSON from Settings > Export Data). Store in VAULT_DIR/ChatExports/. Parse and display conversation list with titles, dates, preview. Full-text search across all conversations. Tag conversations with project names.
acceptance_criteria: Can import Claude JSON export files
Displays conversation list with title, date, preview
Full-text search works across conversations
Can tag conversations with project names
Tagged conversations cross-reference with project cards

TASK-015.title: Multi-vault support (VAULT_DIRS array)
TASK-015.description: Add VAULT_DIRS env var (comma-separated list of absolute paths) that extends VAULT_DIR. Backend TODO extractor, deadline reader, and wiki ingest all scan every configured vault directory. Fallback: if VAULT_DIRS not set, behavior identical to current VAULT_DIR-only mode. Enables reading from Apple Notes exports, a second Obsidian vault, Logseq, etc. ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â any folder of markdown files.
acceptance_criteria: VAULT_DIRS=path1,path2 in .env.example with documentation
TODO extractor scans all configured vault dirs
Deadline reader scans all configured vault dirs for deadlines.md
Graceful skip + warning log for non-existent configured dirs
All existing tests still pass with single VAULT_DIR
Path traversal protection applied to each configured dir
New tests for multi-vault todo and deadline extraction

TASK-016.title: LLM Wiki core: schema, ingest, index, log
TASK-016.description: Build the LLM Wiki infrastructure inside VAULT_DIR/Wiki/. Three layers: raw sources (user's existing notes ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â immutable), wiki (Ollama-generated markdown pages), schema (Wiki/SCHEMA.md ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â conventions seeded from Karpathy pattern via kytmanov/obsidian-llm-wiki-local). Implement: POST /api/wiki/ingest {sourcePath} ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â reads source, Ollama extracts key info and creates/updates wiki pages, updates Wiki/index.md, appends to Wiki/log.md. GET /api/wiki/index ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â returns parsed index. GET /api/wiki/pages ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â lists all wiki pages with metadata. Wiki/ dir auto-created on first ingest. References: kytmanov/obsidian-llm-wiki-local (Ollama + Obsidian wikilink patterns), Astro-Han/karpathy-llm-wiki (schema and workflow).
acceptance_criteria: Wiki/ directory and SCHEMA.md created on first ingest
POST /api/wiki/ingest accepts { sourcePath } and creates/updates wiki pages
Each ingest updates Wiki/index.md catalog and appends to Wiki/log.md
GET /api/wiki/index returns array of pages with summaries and source counts
GET /api/wiki/pages lists all pages with creation date and source count
Source path validated: must be inside VAULT_DIR or PROJECTS_DIR (traversal protection)
Graceful no-op when Ollama unavailable
Obsidian-compatible wikilinks [[Page Name]] in generated markdown
Unit tests for schema seeding, ingest orchestration, index parsing, log appending

TASK-017.title: LLM Wiki query + lint + dashboard panel
TASK-017.description: Add query and lint operations to the wiki. POST /api/wiki/query {question} ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â Ollama reads index.md + retrieves relevant pages by keyword match, synthesizes answer with page citations. POST /api/wiki/lint ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â checks: orphan pages (no inbound links), stale pages (source file newer than wiki page mtime), concepts mentioned but lacking a page, missing cross-references. Returns structured report with health_score 0-100. Frontend: WikiPanel component ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â shows when Wiki/ dir exists. Search box for queries (500ms debounce), lint health badge (green/amber/red), page list with last-updated, ingest button that accepts a file path.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/wiki/query returns { answer, citations: [pageName] }
POST /api/wiki/lint returns { orphans, stale, gaps, health_score }
WikiPanel renders in dashboard when Wiki/ directory exists
Query search box debounced 500ms, shows cited page names
Lint health badge: green (>80), amber (50-80), red (<50)
Ingest button accepts a source path and triggers POST /api/wiki/ingest
Unit tests for query synthesis, lint logic, and health score calculation

TASK-018.title: Self-learning: gap analysis + resource recommendations
TASK-018.description: Extend wiki lint with gap analysis and resource discovery. POST /api/wiki/gaps ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â Ollama reads Wiki/index.md + active project state files to identify: (1) knowledge topics mentioned in active projects but absent from wiki, (2) wiki pages marked as seedling or with no source. For each gap, query DuckDuckGo Instant Answer API (no key required) for top 3 YouTube/article results. Write prioritized gap list to VAULT_DIR/Wiki/gaps.md. Extend notification-service.ts: weekly ntfy notification listing top 3 gaps. Frontend: GapList component under WikiPanel ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â shows gaps with resource links and an Add to Inbox button that prepends - [ ] [date] Learn: {topic} to inbox.md.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/wiki/gaps returns { gaps: [{ topic, priority, resources: [{ title, url, type }] }] }
Gaps ranked by cross-reference frequency in active project state files
Resources fetched via DuckDuckGo Instant Answer API (zero API keys)
gaps.md written to VAULT_DIR/Wiki/gaps.md
Weekly ntfy notification fires once per week with top 3 gaps
Add to Inbox button creates correct inbox.md entry
GapList renders under WikiPanel when gaps.md exists
Unit tests for gap extraction, resource formatting, and inbox write

TASK-019.title: Multi-account Claude chat sync
TASK-019.description: Extend TASK-014 chat export viewer to support multiple Claude accounts. Change flat scan (ChatExports/*.json) to recursive (ChatExports/**/*.json) ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â subfolder name becomes the account label. Both flat exports (backward compatible, account = 'default') and subfolder-organized exports (ChatExports/Personal/, ChatExports/Work/) supported. Update chat-export-parser.ts to add account field to ConversationMeta. Update ChatExplorer frontend to show account badge and filter dropdown.
acceptance_criteria: Existing flat exports in ChatExports/ still work (account = 'default')
Subfolder exports scanned: ChatExports/AccountName/*.json all loaded
ConversationMeta type includes account: string field
ChatExplorer shows account badge on conversation list items
Filter dropdown allows showing/hiding conversations by account
All 23 existing TASK-014 tests still pass
New tests for recursive scan, account derivation, and filter logic

TASK-026.title: Git activity log across all projects
TASK-026.description: For each subdirectory in PROJECTS_DIR that contains a .git folder, run: git -C <dir> log --since=90.days --format=%ai|%s. Parse output to extract date and subject. Aggregate to: {date: commit_count} map for heatmap, plus per-project last-commit info. Expose GET /api/git-activity returning {heatmap:{[date:string]:number}, projects:[{name,lastCommit,commitCount}]}. Add GitActivityPanel: 90-day contribution heatmap using CSS grid (no d3), each cell colored by commit count (0=bg-gray-800, 1-2=bg-green-900, 3-5=bg-green-700, 6+=bg-green-500). Hover shows date and count. Below heatmap: per-project row with last commit message.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/git-activity returns heatmap data
Only scans dirs with .git folder
Heatmap CSS grid renders correctly
Per-project last commit shown
Tests for git log parser

TASK-027.title: Obsidian Canvas reader
TASK-027.description: Scan VAULT_DIR recursively for *.canvas files (JSON Canvas spec: {nodes:[{id,type,text,x,y,width,height}], edges:[{id,fromNode,toNode}]}). For each canvas, extract node count, edge count, and first 3 node texts as preview. Expose GET /api/canvases returning array of {filename, nodeCounts, edgeCount, preview}. Add CanvasPanel to dashboard showing canvas cards. Each card: canvas name, N nodes, N edges, preview text snippets. Also expose POST /api/canvases/:filename/add-node accepting {text, color} to append a new node (write-back allowed ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â canvas files are in vault). This makes it usable as an MCP action from Claude.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/canvases returns list of canvas metadata
JSON Canvas spec correctly parsed
CanvasPanel renders canvas cards with preview
POST add-node appends node to canvas file correctly
Path traversal check on :filename param
Tests for canvas parser and add-node

TASK-025.title: Weekly review generator + daily context
TASK-025.description: Two features in one: (1) Daily context: GET /api/daily-context returns today's deadlines (from existing /api/deadlines), calendar events (from TASK-020 if available), 3 random vault notes for resurfacing. DailyPanel renders this in a card on the dashboard homepage. (2) Weekly review: POST /api/weekly-review (or auto-triggered on Sunday startup). Gathers data: git commits this week (TASK-026), todos closed, articles saved (TASK-022), deadlines next week. Calls Ollama to generate a 200-word markdown summary. Saves to VAULT_DIR/DailyNotes/YYYY-WXX-weekly-review.md. Sends ntfy notification with summary link.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/daily-context returns deadlines + events + random notes
DailyPanel renders on dashboard
POST /api/weekly-review generates Ollama summary
Weekly review saved to vault correctly
ntfy notification sent with weekly review
Tests for daily context assembly

TASK-022.title: Article/bookmark reading tracker
TASK-022.description: Parse two sources and merge. (1) Chrome bookmarks: read AppData\Local\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Bookmarks JSON file (tree structure with roots.bookmark_bar.children etc). Extract all bookmark nodes with name and url. (2) VAULT_DIR/Resources/ReadingLog.md: parse lines matching '- [ ] date | [title](url)' and '- [x] date | [title](url)'. Merge and deduplicate by URL. Expose GET /api/reading?status=all|read|unread. Run Ollama nomic-embed-text-style topic grouping on titles (use keyword extraction since embedding would be overkill ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â just top 3 words from title). Add ReadingPanel with read/unread split and topic clusters. CHROME_BOOKMARKS_PATH defaults to the platform path, overridable via .env.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/reading returns merged bookmarks + reading log items
Chrome bookmarks tree correctly flattened
ReadingLog.md both [ ] and [x] states parsed
Topic clusters shown in ReadingPanel
Graceful if Chrome bookmarks file missing
Tests for both parsers

TASK-028.title: Spaced repetition note resurfacing
TASK-028.description: Track review history in VAULT_DIR/Resources/review-log.json ({[filepath:string]: lastReviewedISO|null}). On startup, scan vault and add any new notes. GET /api/review-queue returns up to 10 notes with status: never_reviewed (null), overdue_30d, overdue_60d, overdue_90d. Notes are scored by: never reviewed first, then by how overdue they are. POST /api/review-log {filePath} marks as reviewed (updates review-log.json). Add ReviewPanel to dashboard: show top note from queue with title + first 100 chars. Buttons: 'Mark Reviewed' and 'Skip (reshuffle)'. Random resurface button pulls random note regardless of queue.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/review-queue returns sorted overdue notes
POST /api/review-log correctly updates review-log.json
ReviewPanel renders with mark/skip buttons
Path traversal check for filePath in POST body
review-log.json initialized on first run
Tests for overdue scoring logic

TASK-021.title: YouTube watch history (Google Takeout)
TASK-021.description: Parse Google Takeout watch-history.json (path: YOUTUBE_HISTORY_PATH in .env). File is a JSON array with fields: title, titleUrl, subtitles (array with name=channel), time. Extract title, channel, watch timestamp. Group by month and deduplicate. Expose GET /api/youtube-history. Add MediaPanel component showing last 30 days of watches and top 5 channels. Also write VAULT_DIR/Resources/YouTube-Takeout-Setup.md guide explaining: takeout.google.com ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ select YouTube ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ deselect all ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ select watch-history ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ download ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ copy watch-history.json to the configured path.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/youtube-history returns parsed history grouped by month
Top channels correctly extracted from subtitles field
MediaPanel renders last 30 days of watches
Setup guide written to VAULT_DIR/Resources/YouTube-Takeout-Setup.md
Graceful empty state when YOUTUBE_HISTORY_PATH not set
Tests for watch history parser

TASK-020.title: Google Calendar OAuth2 integration
TASK-020.description: OAuth2 read-only Google Calendar integration. Create Google Cloud project, implement OAuth2 flow (GET /api/calendar/auth + /api/calendar/callback), store tokens in data/calendar-tokens.json (gitignored). Expose GET /api/calendar returning today + next 7 days events. Add CalendarPanel component showing time blocks and free gaps. Detect free gaps when stale projects exist and show suggestion chip. New .env vars: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/calendar returns events array
OAuth2 flow works (auth ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ callback ÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢ token stored)
CalendarPanel renders today's events and free gaps
Free gap + stale project => suggestion chip shown
data/calendar-tokens.json is gitignored
Tests for calendar route with mocked Google API

TASK-029.title: Browser web clipper Chrome extension
TASK-029.description: Build a Chrome extension (manifest v3) in src/extension/ directory. Files: manifest.json (name=Cortex Clipper, version=1.0.0, permissions=[activeTab], action={popup}), popup.html (shows current tab title + url pre-filled, optional note textarea, Clip button), popup.js (reads current tab via chrome.tabs.query, POSTs to http://localhost:3001/api/capture). POST body: {text: '[title](url) - note', source: 'web-clipper', tags: ['clipped']}. Icons: generate simple SVG icons at 16/48/128px using inline SVG data URIs in manifest. Include src/extension/README.md with developer mode install instructions. Add CORS allow for chrome-extension:// origin in server. No external API calls from extension.
acceptance_criteria: src/extension/manifest.json valid manifest v3
Popup shows current tab URL and title auto-filled
POST to /api/capture succeeds from extension
Server CORS allows chrome-extension:// origin
README.md explains developer mode install
No external network calls from extension

TASK-024.title: Knowledge graph visualizer (D3.js wikilinks)
TASK-024.description: Parse all .md files in VAULT_DIR for [[wikilinks]] (regex: \[\[([^\]|#]+)\]\]). Build nodes+edges. Nodes: each unique note filename (no extension), colored by PARA folder (Inbox=slate, Projects=blue, Areas=green, Resources=amber, Archive=gray, Wiki=purple). Edges: sourceÃƒÆ’Ã‚Â¢ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã‚Â ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â€žÂ¢target pairs. Expose GET /api/knowledge-graph returning {nodes:[{id,label,folder,linkCount}], edges:[{source,target}]}. Add KnowledgeGraph component using D3.js (npm install d3 @types/d3). Force-directed layout, click node opens obsidian://open?vault=SecondBrain&file=noteName. Slider for node limit (default 200).
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/knowledge-graph returns nodes+edges
Nodes colored by PARA folder correctly
D3 force graph renders without crashing on large vaults
Click node opens Obsidian deep link
Slider limits rendered nodes
Tests for wikilink parser

TASK-023.title: Full RAG chat interface over all data
TASK-023.description: Build conversational Ollama chat over all Cortex data. On each POST /api/chat/query: (1) keyword-extract the query (split, lowercase, filter stopwords), (2) search all data sources for relevant chunks ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â vault notes (scan markdown), projects state files, chat exports, wiki entries (TASK-016 output), reading list ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â score by keyword overlap, take top-20 chunks, (3) build context string from top chunks capped at 6000 chars, (4) call Ollama llama3.1:8b with context + question, (5) stream response as SSE. Add BrainChat component: full-panel chat UI with input box and streamed message display. Maintain session-only conversation history array (not persisted).
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/chat/query returns SSE stream with Ollama response
Context assembled from all data sources
BrainChat component renders with streaming
Session history maintained (reset on refresh is fine)
Tests for context assembly and keyword scoring

TASK-030.title: Cortex MCP server ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â expose backend as Claude Desktop tool server
TASK-030.description: Implement an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes Cortex data as tools callable by Claude Desktop. Uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. Tools: list_todos, get_deadlines, list_projects, search_notes, add_capture, get_daily_context, search_wiki, get_reading_log.
acceptance_criteria: server/mcp-server.ts starts standalone on a configurable port (default 3002)
8 tools registered: list_todos, get_deadlines, list_projects, search_notes, add_capture, get_daily_context, search_wiki, get_reading_log
Claude Desktop can connect via mcp.json config (stdio or sse transport)
All tools return typed JSON with error handling
package.json script: mcp:dev runs the MCP server
Tests for all 8 tools (mocked filesystem)

TASK-031.title: Fix mcp-tools.test.ts stale hardcoded dates
TASK-031.description: Two tests in server/lib/mcp-tools.test.ts fail because they hardcode '2026-04-19' in assertions for get_daily_context. Replace all hardcoded date strings in test assertions (and any mock heatmap keys using that date) with dynamic: const today = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]. No production code changes needed.

No hardcoded date strings remain in mcp-tools.test.ts assertions
Dynamic date used for both get_daily_context test assertions and any heatmap mock data keys

TASK-032.title: Multi-page dashboard routing restructure
TASK-032.description: Restructure the single-page App.tsx into a navigatable multi-route app using react-router-dom v6. Install react-router-dom. Add backend endpoint GET /api/projects/:slug (URL-encoded project name) returning full project detail: state file content, scoped TODOs (from existing extractor filtered by project path), AI summary if Ollama available. Add POST /api/deadlines to append a new deadline line to VAULT_DIR/Deadlines/deadlines.md (format: - [ ] YYYY-MM-DD | Description | optional-tag) and DELETE /api/deadlines/:id to remove a deadline line by ID. Create src/components/Navigation.tsx sidebar with links and active route indicator. Create 6 page components in src/pages/: HomePage.tsx (DailyPanel + CalendarPanel + GitActivityPanel + ReviewPanel + StatusOverview), ProjectsPage.tsx (existing ProjectCard grid), ProjectDetailPage.tsx (GET /api/projects/:slug ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â state content, scoped TODOs, AI summary, Open in VS Code button), DeadlinesPage.tsx (DeadlineTimeline + add form + delete buttons), KnowledgePage.tsx (KnowledgeGraph + WikiPanel + CanvasPanel), LearningPage.tsx (GapList + ReadingPanel + BrainChat + MediaPanel + ChatExplorer). Update App.tsx to use BrowserRouter wrapping a persistent layout (Navigation sidebar + QuickCapture top bar) and Routes for each page. Routes: / (HomePage), /projects (ProjectsPage), /projects/:slug (ProjectDetailPage), /deadlines (DeadlinesPage), /knowledge (KnowledgePage), /learning (LearningPage).
acceptance_criteria: npm install react-router-dom succeeds, no new TypeScript errors
All 6 routes navigate correctly via Navigation sidebar links
Navigation component highlights active route
GET /api/projects/:slug returns project detail with scoped TODOs and AI summary
POST /api/deadlines appends correctly formatted line to deadlines.md
DELETE /api/deadlines/:id removes line from deadlines.md (path traversal protection on file write)
DeadlinesPage shows add form (date + description + optional tag) and delete button per item

All previously passing tests still pass (no regressions)
New tests for GET /api/projects/:slug, POST /api/deadlines, DELETE /api/deadlines/:id

TASK-033.title: Single unified startup command (frontend + backend + Ollama)
TASK-033.description: Add scripts/start-ollama.js ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â a Node.js CJS script that checks whether Ollama is already listening on port 11434 (using net.connect). If not running, spawns 'ollama serve' and keeps the process alive. If already running, logs '[ollama] already running' and exits 0. Update package.json: add dev:ollama script ('node scripts/start-ollama.js') and update dev script to run frontend + backend + ollama concurrently with --kill-others-on-fail false so Ollama exiting early never kills the other two. No tests needed (dev-only startup script).
acceptance_criteria: npm run dev starts frontend (:5173), backend (:3001), AND Ollama
If Ollama is already running, command detects this and logs it without error
Frontend and backend continue even if Ollama exits early
scripts/start-ollama.js created with port-check logic
concurrently uses --kill-others-on-fail false
TASK-033.engineer_notes: Implementation complete (commit a07f412). All 610 tests pass. Pre-existing TypeScript errors in server/lib/mcp-tools.ts (TASK-030) prevented full type-check validation but are unrelated to this task's changes.

TASK-034.title: Electron desktop application packaging
TASK-034.description: Package Cortex as a full native Electron desktop app with system tray, titlebar, taskbar icon. Install electron and electron-builder. Create electron/main.ts (main process): dev mode loads http://localhost:5173 after waiting for Vite + backend to be ready; production mode starts Express as child process and loads from it. Spawn Ollama check/start logic. System tray icon with Open and Quit menu. Window close hides to tray (does not quit). On quit: kill all child processes. Create electron/preload.ts (minimal). Add separate tsconfig for electron (electron/tsconfig.json). Add package.json scripts: electron:dev (concurrently runs dev:backend + dev:ollama + electron in watch mode), electron:build (runs vite build + tsc server + electron-builder). Add electron-builder config in package.json for Windows NSIS installer. In production, Express serves the Vite dist/ output as static files AND the /api/* routes, so Electron BrowserWindow loads http://localhost:3001 only.
acceptance_criteria: npm run electron:dev opens a native Electron window showing the Cortex dashboard
System tray icon visible with Open and Quit options
Closing the window hides it to tray rather than quitting
npm run electron:build produces a Windows .exe installer in release/
Ollama is started automatically when Electron app launches
All child processes (backend, Ollama) are killed when app quits
No TypeScript errors in electron/ code
TASK-034.engineer_notes: Implementation complete (commit 33b3e3a). Electron main and preload compile to .cjs files as required by package.json type: module. All 608 tests passing (2 pre-existing failures in git-activity-parser and DeadlineTimeline unrelated to Electron changes). npm run electron:compile verified.

TASK-035.title: Fix review queue ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â exclude project junctions and non-note files
TASK-035.description: The vault's Projects/ folder contains NTFS junction points to _Projects/*. glob follows these junctions, causing all project markdown files to appear in the spaced-repetition review queue. Fix syncNewNotes in server/lib/review-log.ts to exclude Projects/, Wiki/, ChatExports/, and DailyNotes/ from the glob scan. Also ensure the ReviewPanel component shows a meaningful empty state if no vault notes exist.

ReviewPanel shows empty state when no overdue vault notes

Commit with descriptive message

TASK-041.title: Zen/Focus Mode with Pomodoro Timer
TASK-041.description: Add /focus/:slug route. Shows: project name, filtered TODOs for that project only (from existing GET /api/todos filtered client-side by project), a Pomodoro timer (25min work / 5min break / 15min long break, cycling). Timer uses useState + setInterval with proper cleanup. Minimal UI ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â dark background, large timer digits, todo list. NavBar hidden in focus mode. 'Exit Focus' button returns to previous page via useNavigate(-1). Audio notification on timer end using Web Audio API (synthesized beep, no external files).

Audio beep on timer completion (Web Audio API, no files)

TASK-042.title: In-App Command Palette (Ctrl+K)
TASK-042.description: Replace the simple Ctrl+K focus behavior in QuickCapture with a full command palette using the 'cmdk' React library (npm install cmdk). On Cmd/Ctrl+K, show the cmdk modal overlay with search input. Fuzzy-match against: page routes ('Go to Projects', 'Go to Deadlines', 'Go to Knowledge', 'Go to Learning'), project names ('Open {projectName}' ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â‚¬Â Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ navigates to /projects/:slug), actions ('Run Wiki Lint', 'Generate Weekly Review', 'Capture to Inbox'). Results grouped by type (Navigate, Projects, Actions). Typing 'Capture: {text}' prefix direct-captures to inbox via POST /api/capture. Uses existing react-router useNavigate for navigation, existing API endpoints for actions. Escape closes palette. Create src/components/CommandPalette.tsx using cmdk's Command component and wire into App.tsx.

TASK-043.title: Automated State Diffing (weekly git-summary per project)
TASK-043.description: Add POST /api/projects/:slug/auto-summary ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â runs 'git log --since=7.days --stat' for the project directory, feeds the diff summary to Ollama with a prompt: 'Summarize this week\'s development activity in 3-4 sentences. What was worked on? What changed? Be factual, only use the git log below.' Writes result to PROJECTS_DIR/{project}/.cortex-weekly-summary.md (never overwrites state files). Add weekly auto-trigger in notification-service.ts on Friday at 5PM (configurable via WEEKLY_SUMMARY_DAY and WEEKLY_SUMMARY_HOUR env vars, defaults: Friday/17). Frontend: show weekly summary in ProjectDetailView if .cortex-weekly-summary.md file exists, displayed as a collapsible 'This Week' section. Graceful no-op when Ollama unavailable or no git history in the project.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/projects/:slug/auto-summary generates Ollama summary from git diffs
Summary written to PROJECTS_DIR/{project}/.cortex-weekly-summary.md
Weekly auto-trigger in notification-service.ts (Sunday, same schedule as weekly review)
ProjectDetailView displays weekly summary when file exists
Graceful no-op when Ollama unavailable or no git history
Never overwrites state files (only writes .cortex-weekly-summary.md)

Tests for git diff parsing and summary prompt construction

TASK-044.title: Velocity Tracking (daily snapshots + trend chart)
TASK-044.description: Create server/lib/velocity-tracker.ts ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â on server startup, use 'simple-git' library (npm install simple-git) to count commits across PROJECTS_DIR, and parse markdown files for newly checked - [x] boxes. Append daily snapshot to VAULT_DIR/.cortex-velocity.json: {date: 'YYYY-MM-DD', todosOpen: number, todosClosed: number, commitsToday: number}. Deduplicates by date (only one entry per day, updates if already exists for today). GET /api/velocity returns last 90 days of snapshots. Frontend: VelocityPanel component on HomePage ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â use Recharts (npm install recharts) for a clean bar chart showing todos closed/day and commits/day as two colored series over time. Trend indicator arrow comparing this week's average vs last week's average. EXTENSION: Add a riskScore field to GET /api/deadlines response. Formula: risk = remainingTodos / (avgTodosCompletedPerWeek * weeksUntilDeadline). Values: >1.0 = likely miss (red badge), 0.7-1.0 = tight (amber badge), <0.7 = on track (green badge). Display as a small color-coded risk indicator on each deadline row in DeadlineTimeline. Requires correlating project deadlines with project TODO counts and velocity data.
acceptance_criteria: Daily snapshot appended to VAULT_DIR/.cortex-velocity.json on server startup
Deduplicates by date (one entry per day)
GET /api/velocity returns array of daily snapshots (last 90 days)
VelocityPanel renders pure SVG line chart with two series (todos, commits)
Trend arrow shows week-over-week direction (up/down/flat)
No external chart library (pure SVG path elements)
Chart auto-scales y-axis to data range
Tests for snapshot recording, deduplication, and trend calculation
GET /api/deadlines includes riskScore field per deadline (float, higher = more risk)
Risk formula: remainingTodos / (avgTodosPerWeek * weeksUntilDeadline)
DeadlineTimeline shows color-coded risk badge (red >1.0, amber 0.7-1.0, green <0.7)
Graceful when no velocity data available (risk = null, no badge shown)

TASK-045.title: Kanban Triage Board (checkbox ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â‚¬Â Ã¢â‚¬â„¢ drag-and-drop columns)
TASK-045.description: Extend TODO extractor to recognize - [/] as 'in progress' state (new type alongside unchecked/checked). Add GET /api/kanban grouping TODOs into three columns: todo (- [ ]), doing (- [/]), done (- [x]). Add PATCH /api/todos/:id/status accepting {status: 'todo'|'doing'|'done'} that rewrites the checkbox marker in the source file ([ ] for todo, [/] for doing, [x] for done). Frontend: /kanban route with three columns. Use HTML5 drag-and-drop API (no external library) ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â drag todo card between columns triggers PATCH. Cards show text + project badge + file chip. Filter dropdown by project. NavBar includes Kanban link.
acceptance_criteria: - [/] recognized as 'in_progress' type by TODO extractor
GET /api/kanban returns {todo: Todo[], doing: Todo[], done: Todo[]}
PATCH /api/todos/:id/status writes correct checkbox marker to source file
Status transitions: todoÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â‚¬Â Ã¢â‚¬ÂdoingÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â‚¬Â Ã¢â‚¬Âdone (all directions allowed)

Write-back doesn't corrupt file content
Filter dropdown by project name
NavBar includes Kanban link
/kanban route renders correctly

TASK-046.title: Local Semantic Search (nomic-embed-text + SQLite-vss)
TASK-046.description: Install better-sqlite3 and sqlite-vss. Create server/lib/embedding-index.ts ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â on first run, scan vault + projects for all .md files, chunk into ~500-char passages (split by paragraph/heading boundary), call Ollama nomic-embed-text for each chunk, store embeddings in data/cortex-embeddings.db (gitignored). Incremental updates: on server startup, compare file mtimes against stored mtimes, re-embed only changed files. Add GET /api/search?q={query}&mode=semantic|keyword (default semantic). Semantic mode: embed the query, compute cosine similarity against stored vectors, return top-20 results. Update rag-engine.ts to use semantic scoring when embeddings available, fall back to keyword when not. Frontend: add search mode toggle (Semantic/Keyword) to BrainChat header. Log indexing progress to console during initial build. First-run indexing is async and non-blocking (server responds to requests while indexing in background).
acceptance_criteria: Initial embedding index built on first startup (async, non-blocking)
Progress logged to console during indexing (N/total files)
Incremental updates on subsequent startups (only changed files re-embedded)
GET /api/search?mode=semantic returns cosine-similarity ranked results
GET /api/search?mode=keyword returns existing keyword-based results
RAG chat (rag-engine.ts) uses semantic scoring when index available
Fallback to keyword scoring when Ollama or embeddings unavailable
data/cortex-embeddings.db in .gitignore
Chunking splits by paragraph/heading boundaries (~500 chars each)
Tests for chunking logic, incremental update detection, and cosine similarity

TASK-047.title: Extend MCP Server with 3 new tools
TASK-047.description: Add 3 new MCP tools to server/lib/mcp-tools.ts: (1) run_wiki_lint ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â wraps lintWiki from wiki-manager.ts, returns health score + orphans/stale/gaps arrays, (2) generate_weekly_review ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â triggers weekly review generation (reuses logic from server/routes/weekly.ts), returns generated summary text, (3) get_project_detail ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â accepts project name/slug, returns project state file content + scoped TODOs + AI summary (reuses logic from GET /api/projects/:slug). Register all 3 in server/mcp-server.ts. Add Zod schemas for inputs. Update mcp-config.example.json tool count comment. Update existing MCP tests pattern.
acceptance_criteria: run_wiki_lint tool registered and returns {healthScore, orphans, stale, gaps}
generate_weekly_review tool registered and returns {summary, savedTo}
get_project_detail tool registered and returns {name, status, stateContent, todos, aiSummary}

TASK-048.title: Electron Global Shortcut Overlay (Ctrl+Shift+Space)
TASK-048.description: In electron/main.ts, register globalShortcut for Ctrl+Shift+Space (configurable via GLOBAL_SHORTCUT env var). On trigger, show a small frameless, always-on-top BrowserWindow (500x80px) centered on screen with a single input field. The overlay window loads a minimal HTML page (electron/overlay.html ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â not the full dashboard) containing just the input + submit logic. On Enter: POST to http://localhost:3001/api/capture with {text: input}, show brief green checkmark, then hide window after 500ms. On Escape: hide window. On blur: hide window. Keyboard shortcut registered on app ready, unregistered on will-quit. The overlay input auto-focuses on show. Style: dark background matching Cortex theme (#1f2937), rounded corners, subtle shadow.

electron/overlay.html created with minimal standalone UI

TASK-036.title: Fix project summaries ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â use state-file content, not hallucinated AI
TASK-036.description: Project cards show wacky AI-generated summaries because: (1) Ollama's llama3.1:8b model may not be pulled yet (causing 404 API errors), (2) the AI prompt doesn't constrain well enough. Fix: In ProjectCard.tsx, show the project.summary field (extracted from state file by scanner) as the primary card description. Show the AI summary only as a supplementary 'AI insight' section below if available. In server/routes/ai.ts and ollama-client.ts, add a clear log message when Ollama returns 404: 'Model not pulled ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â run: ollama pull llama3.1:8b'. Update the Ollama prompt to be more specific: 'You are reading a project state file. In 2-3 sentences, describe where the developer left off. Be factual ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â only use what is in the file below. Do not invent details.'
acceptance_criteria: ProjectCard shows state-file summary as primary content
AI summary shown only as secondary section when Ollama is available
Ollama 404 logs clear message suggesting ollama pull

Tests updated to match new ProjectCard rendering

TASK-037.title: Wiki ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â Scan Projects button to bulk-ingest project README/state files
TASK-037.description: The wiki has no onboarding ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â users must manually type file paths to ingest. Add: (1) Backend: POST /api/wiki/ingest-projects ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â scans PROJECTS_DIR, finds each project's state file (using same priority order: agent_state.md > Agent_State.json > state.md > Status.md > README.md), and ingests each into the wiki. Returns { ingested: number, errors: string[] }. (2) Frontend: WikiPanel.tsx ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â add a 'Scan Projects' button that calls this endpoint, shows progress ('Ingesting N projects...'), and displays result summary on completion. The button should only appear when Ollama is available (check /api/ai/status).

TASK-038.title: Fix Obsidian deep links ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â configurable VAULT_NAME env var
TASK-038.description: All obsidian:// deep links are hardcoded with vault=SecondBrain, but the registered vault name in Obsidian may differ. Fix: add VAULT_NAME to .env.example (default: SecondBrain). In all backend routes that generate obsidian:// URLs (review.ts, reading.ts, any others), use process.env.VAULT_NAME. In all frontend components that build obsidian:// links (ReviewPanel, ReadingPanel, CanvasPanel, etc.), fetch the vault name from a new GET /api/config endpoint that returns { vaultName, projectsDir }. TASK-049.title: Context Switch Protocol (cognitive disengagement)
TASK-049.description: When navigating from one project detail page to a different project detail page (via ProjectsPage, NavBar, or any route change from /projects/:slug to a different /projects/:slug), intercept with a full-screen 'Context Unload' modal. Three fields: 'What were you doing?' (textarea), 'What\'s blocking?' (textarea), 'What\'s next?' (textarea). On submit: POST to a new endpoint POST /api/projects/:slug/context-dump that appends the brain dump to the outgoing project's state.md (or a .cortex-context.md file in the project dir). Then navigate to the new project. 'Skip' button bypasses the modal. Persist skip preference in localStorage. Integrate naturally with Focus Mode (TASK-041) ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â exiting focus mode also triggers the unload if switching projects. Does NOT trigger for non-project navigation (e.g. deadlines page to knowledge page).

decisions.title: Code location
decisions.decision: Brain2 repo IS the dashboard ÃƒÂ¢Ã¢â€šÂ¬Ã¢â‚¬Â reads vault/projects paths via .env config
decisions.reason: Cleaner than embedding inside Obsidian vault. Single repo, standard structure, configurable paths.

decisions.title: Package structure
decisions.decision: Single package with concurrently for dev
decisions.reason: Simpler for personal tool. One package.json, concurrently runs Vite + Express.

decisions.title: AI provider
decisions.reason: User chose Claude API only. No need for provider abstraction on a personal tool.

changelog.description: Implemented command palette with cmdk library - Ctrl+K opens fuzzy search for navigation, projects, and actions

current_task.title: Automated State Diffing (weekly git-summary per project)
